title: CutandTag_Alignment_QC: A reproducible Snakemake workflow for alignment, quality control, and signal generation from CUT&Tag sequencing data
description: CutandTag_Alignment_QC is a Snakemake workflow for initial processing and quality assessment of paired-end CUT&Tag sequencing data. The workflow performs optional raw FASTQ quality control with FastQC, MultiQC, and FastQ Screen; paired-end read alignment with Bowtie2; SAM/BAM conversion, sorting, and indexing with SAMtools; optional fragment-coordinate duplicate capping; optional random downsampling using configurable target-selection modes and reproducible or randomly generated seeds; generation of raw, CPM-normalized, and target-scaled BigWig tracks with deepTools; per-sample peak calling with MACS2 in paired-end BAM mode; fragment-length analysis; per-sample FRiP calculation; fragment-count correlation; and QC summary plotting. Final analysis BAM selection is determined by the duplicate-capping and downsampling settings. The workflow is designed for Slurm-managed HPC execution with Snakemake and environment modules and is intended for alignment, preprocessing, signal generation, and sample-level QC before downstream replicate-aware analysis.
